name: Herb Crepes with Goat's Cheese Stuffing
recipeInstructions:
(Units: 100 g ó 1/2 oz; 1 dl ó 1/2 fl oz ò/5 cup; 180 oC ó50 oF; 200 oC ô00 oF; 230 oC ô50 oF; 250 oC ô75 oF; 2.5 cm ñ inch)
(Two crepes per person for a first course, serve with the tomato sauce)
1. Break the eggs into a blender or food processor and add the flour, salt, and pepper to taste.
2. Blend, gradually adding the milk and water. Add the snipped chives; if not snipped, they may wrap around the blades. Continue blending until the herbs are finely incorporated.
3. Pour the crepe batter into a bowl and let it stand for half an hour.
4. To cook the crepes, melt about 1½ teaspoons of butter in a crepe or omelette pan and swirl it over the surface.
5. Pour in just enough batter to cover the bottom of the pan, swirling the batter around so that the crepe will be as thin as possible.
6. Cook over moderate heat for about 30 seconds, then turn the crepe over using a small palette knife or spatula and your fingers.
7. As they are cooked, stack the crepes with a piece of greaseproof (wax) paper between each to prevent them from sticking together.
8. For the filling, put the goat's cheese into a food processor with the egg whites and blend until smooth.
9. Put a generous teaspoonful of filling in the middle of each crepe and fold into a fat rectangle.
10. Place the stuffed crepes into an oiled or buttered heatproof dish and brush with melted butter.
11. Bake in a preheated moderate oven at 180°C for 20 minutes.
